tags.name: Scuba Lanka
tags.qa_note: "Scuba Lanka" is the name of a PADI dive shop/operator in Trincomalee (scubalanka.com, padi.com dive-center listing), not a natural dive site. Likely an OSM node tagging the shop's location rather than an actual site. Flagged per CLAUDE.md commercial-business exclusion; needs a maintainer decision on whether to rename to an actual nearby site, or remove.

tags.name: Anchor Point
tags.qa_note: No source confirms literal anchors/chains on the seabed; prior markdown's 'colonial spice trade anchor' narrative was unsupported and has been removed.

tags.name: Cave dive site
tags.qa_note: This node sits ~156m from 'Gala pita gala dive site' and is very likely the same cave/pinnacle feature (the only named cave system in this exact location in any source). Description now treats it as adjoining/likely-identical to Gala Pita Gala rather than inventing a distinct site identity.

tags.name: Gala pita gala dive site
tags.qa_note: Reclassified reef->cave and shore->boat: 4 independent sources (PADI, Sun Diving/diveinsrilanka.com, Pearl Divers/unawatunadive.com, pearldiverslk.com) describe a rock pinnacle with a ~3m natural cave, boat access (10-15 min from Unawatuna), max depth 25m (top of pinnacle ~10m).

tags.name: Conch dive site
tags.depth_range: 14-21m (sources also cite up to 22m in places)
tags.qa_note: Site is the SS Conch wreck near Akurala/Hikkaduwa (~8km from Hikkaduwa town, consistent with coordinates). Sinking date conflict: PADI and divingsrilanka.com say June 2, 1903; archaeology.lk says June 3, 1903 - both retained as a range in the description. Depth also varies 14-21m vs 22m across sources - both noted.

tags.name: Carreg dive site
tags.qa_note: No independent source (Hikkaduwa's published 10-wreck list, Negombo dive-site lists, ScubaBoard, Wikipedia) corroborates a wreck called 'Carreg' anywhere in Sri Lanka. 'Carreg' is a Welsh word for 'rock' used in Welsh (Pembrokeshire/North Wales) dive-site naming; coordinates place this node near Beruwala/Bentota, ~120km from the nearest documented wreck cluster (Hikkaduwa). Likely a mistagged/unverifiable OSM node. Previous markdown's full wreck-penetration narrative was unsupported fabrication and has been removed. Left unvalidated per validation rules (no source = do not mark validated).

tags.name: Goddamahagale dive site
tags.qa_note: Coordinates place this ~7km from Negombo's 'Maha Gala' dive site and within the Negombo 'Gala' cluster (Maha Gala, Mada Gala, Y-Gala, Dakune Gala, Diyamba Gala) - NOT the southern coast/Hikkaduwa location the previous markdown claimed (that description was wrong by ~127km). However, no source was fetched that confirms this exact OSM name as a specific named site, so this fails the 3-independent-source bar for validation. Location corrected from 'southern coast near Hikkaduwa' to 'west coast near Negombo' based on coordinates. entry_type changed reef sites in this Negombo cluster are consistently boat-accessed (Maha Gala, Pakistan Wreck, etc. per zentacle.com), so entry_type updated shore->boat, but site_type/depth left unchanged pending a site-specific source.

tags.name: Unuwatuna Coral Reef
tags.qa_note: Removed 'known_wrecks: SS Rangoon' tag - the SS Rangoon wreck lies at 30-32m near Galle harbor (per pearldiverslk.com), a distinct and much deeper site from this shallow (5-18m) fringing reef; the association was misleading. Marine life (turtles, moray, wrasse) is confirmed at the Unawatuna-bay level across 3 sources but not tied to this exact reef polygon by name, so description now attributes it at the bay/regional level rather than claiming site-exclusive sightings.
